title: SBI PO 2026
meta_description: SBI PO 2026 — Probationary Officer at State Bank of India. Prelims/Mains pattern, salary ₹40-68K/month, eligibility, cutoff data, and how it differs from IBPS PO.

hero.one_liner: Probationary Officer at India's largest bank — SBI PO 2026 offers the best salary, brand value, and career growth in public sector banking
hero.updated_date: Last reviewed: April 2026 • Sources: SBI Careers, IBPS

what_is_it: SBI PO (State Bank of India — Probationary Officer) is one of India's most prestigious banking exams. Unlike IBPS PO which recruits for 11 banks, SBI PO is exclusively for the State Bank of India — India's largest public sector bank with 22,000+ branches, highest market capitalization among Indian banks, and operations across global financial centers.

SBI PO 2026 is expected to release notifications in May-June 2026 with 400-600 vacancies announced. The exam offers a higher starting salary than IBPS PO banks — approximately ₹80,000-82,000/month in-hand depending on posting city. SBI also has superior technology infrastructure, diverse banking operations (investment banking, treasury, international banking), stronger brand value, and lateral transfer opportunities to RBI, SEBI, and NABARD.

The exam has three stages: Prelims (online screening at 10× shortlisting ratio), Mains (comprehensive merit exam with objective + descriptive), and Interview. The pattern resembles IBPS PO but the Data Analysis section is significantly harder, reasoning puzzles are tougher, and the descriptive component (50 marks) carries substantial weight in final selection.

SBI conducts recruitment independently without IBPS involvement. Notifications typically release October-November with Prelims in November-December, Mains in January-February, and Interview in March-April. The recruitment process spans 5-6 months from notification to final offer letter.

key_details.label: Conducting Body
key_details.value: State Bank of India (SBI) — NOT IBPS

key_details.label: Education
key_details.value: Bachelor's degree from recognized university (any stream). Final year students eligible.

key_details.label: Selection
key_details.value: Prelims → Mains → Interview (final merit: 75% Mains + 25% Interview)

key_details.label: Salary
key_details.value: Basic: ₹48,480/month. Total in-hand: ₹80,000–82,000/month depending on city posting.

exam_pattern.description: Online exam, 100 MCQs, 60 minutes with strict sectional timing. Qualifying only — marks don't count in final merit. About 10× candidates shortlisted for Mains based on performance across sections.

Online objective + Descriptive. Objective paper (200 marks, 3 hours) has 4 sections with sectional time limits. Descriptive (50 marks, 30 minutes) — letter writing + essay. Final merit calculated: 75% Mains + 25% Interview.

major_posts.post: Probationary Officer (JMGS-I / Junior Management Grade Scale 1)
major_posts.department: State Bank of India — Assigned by HR at end of training

extra_sections.heading: SBI PO vs IBPS PO — Key Difference
extra_sections.content: SBI PO is conducted by SBI itself; IBPS PO is for 11 other public sector banks. SBI PO has descriptive paper (essay + letter) while IBPS PO doesn't. Otherwise difficulty is similar — most candidates apply for both.

extra_sections.heading: SBI PO Career Growth — From Officer to MD
content: PO (JMGS-I, Entry) → ₹80,000-82,000/month (Year 1-3). After 3-4 years of regular performance and an internal promotion exam, POs are promoted to Manager (MMGS-II, Mid-Management Grade Scale 2) — salary jumps to ₹1,20,000-1,40,000/month.
Manager (MMGS-II, 4-6 years) → Senior Manager (MMGS-III, 6-8 years) — ₹1,40,000-1,80,000/month. Senior Managers handle branch operations, customer portfolios, and staff management.
They're responsible for deposit mobilization, loan portfolios, and branch profitability.

Senior Manager (MMGS-III) → Chief Manager (SMGS-IV, 8-10 years) → AGM (Assistant General Manager, TEGS-V) → DGM (Deputy General Manager, TEGS-VI) → GM (General Manager, TEGS-VII) → CGM (Chief General Manager) → DMD (Deputy Managing Director) → MD (Managing Director).

SBI POs get opportunities for lateral posting in: (1) Treasury Operations — managing forex, bonds, derivatives (salary premium +₹20,000-50,000/month), (2) International Banking — overseas postings in London, New York, Singapore (hardship allowance included), (3) Investment Banking — via SBI Capital Markets subsidiary (higher compensation), (4) Insurance — SBI Life Insurance as channel partners (commission-based income), (5) Digital Banking Innovation — YONO app development teams (tech exposure).

Promotion to RBI, SEBI, NABARD: After 12-15 years of outstanding service, POs can be selected for lateral recruitment to RBI Grade B (equivalent to GM at SBI, but salary ₹1.5 lakh+/month). This opens doors to highest levels of financial administration — Deputy Governor, Governor positions managing India's entire monetary policy.

extra_sections.heading: SBI PO Cutoff Trends & Merit Analysis
Reality Check
extra_sections.content: SBI PO selection ratio is roughly 1 in 200. Around 2,000 vacancies attract 4-5 lakh applicants yearly. Prepare for 6-8 months minimum — attempting without serious preparation wastes your fee and 6 months of application wait.

preparation_tips: Data Analysis & Interpretation is SBI's differentiator — harder than IBPS PO. Practice advanced DI patterns: caselets (information-rich paragraphs), mixed graphs (combining pie chart + bar graph), data sufficiency. Solve 2-3 DI sets daily from Adda247 or Testbook starting Month 3.

Banking Awareness must be SBI-specific — not generic. Study: (1) SBI's latest quarterly results (net profit, NPA ratio, market share), (2) SBI's digital initiatives (YONO app features, digital-only accounts), (3) MD & Chairman names, (4) Recent mergers (SBI merged with associate banks), (5) SBI subsidiary names (SBI Life, SBI General, SBI Funds). Read SBI's investor relations page monthly.

Descriptive paper (50 marks) directly impacts final selection — many candidates with higher mains scores lose here. Practice: (1) One formal letter (150 words) daily — complaint letters, service request letters, policy inquiry letters, (2) One essay (250 words) weekly — topics from economic policy, digital India, financial inclusion, RBI monetary policy, inflation management. Get reviewed by mentors.

If preparing for IBPS PO, you're 90% ready for SBI PO. The extra 10% = harder DI + SBI-specific awareness. Appear for both exams — 2 attempts = higher probability of selection. Many toppers clear both.

faqs.question: Is SBI PO harder than IBPS PO?
faqs.answer: Slightly harder. Overall pattern is similar, but SBI PO Mains has tougher Data Analysis questions, more complex reasoning puzzles, and the descriptive component (50 marks) is more important than IBPS (25 marks). Competition is also fiercer — 20+ lakh apply for 400-600 vacancies vs 15-20 lakh for 4,500 in IBPS PO. However, if you clear IBPS PO, you're competitive for SBI PO too.

faqs.question: What's the difference between SBI PO salary and IBPS PO salary?
faqs.answer: SBI PO starting salary: ₹48,480 basic → ₹80,000-82,000 in-hand. IBPS PO: ₹42,020-45,000 basic → ₹65,000-75,000 in-hand. SBI pays ~₹10,000-15,000 more monthly. However, IBPS banks vary — HDFC Bank (private) and Bank of Baroda (PSU) have different pay scales. SBI offers superior job security and brand value.

faqs.question: Can I appear for both SBI PO and IBPS PO in the same year?
faqs.answer: Yes! They're conducted by different bodies at different times. SBI PO: Aug-Sep 2026. IBPS PO: Oct-Dec 2026. Many aspiring bankers appear for both. The syllabus is 90% common, so you save preparation time. If you clear both, you can choose your allocated bank or SBI.

faqs.question: What happens after I'm selected? Is there a probation period?
faqs.answer: Yes — 2 years probation after selection. During probation: (1) Posted at a branch for initial training (6 months), (2) Assigned to various departments (cash, deposits, loans, customer service, digital banking) for hands-on learning, (3) Undergo online induction training courses, (4) Clear a probation exam after 12-15 months. Salary is paid fully during probation — no reduction. After clearing probation, you're confirmed as a regular PO.

faqs.question: Where will I be posted after selection?
faqs.answer: SBI has all-India posting liability. After training, you can be posted at any of SBI's 22,000+ branches across India. Initial postings are typically: (1) Tier-2 / Tier-3 cities for first 2-3 years, (2) Metropolitan cities after 4-5 years based on transfer policy. You can apply for specific zones during recruitment — preference is considered but not guaranteed.

faqs.question: How much time should I dedicate to SBI PO preparation?
faqs.answer: Minimum 6-8 months of full-time preparation required. If working, 4-5 hours daily for 10-12 months. The exam tests speed and accuracy — MCQs require 1-1.5 minutes each. Complete one full-length mock every week starting Month 4. Solve previous year papers (2018-2024) — patterns repeat frequently.

faqs.question: Are there separate cutoffs for general and reserved categories?
faqs.answer: Yes. Prelims and Mains have separate cutoffs for General, OBC, SC, ST, and PwBD categories. SC/ST typically have 5-15 marks lower cutoff. Interview is common for all. However, selection is strictly merit-based within each category — you're ranked against your category, not against all candidates.

faqs.question: What is the age limit relaxation for SC/ST/OBC?
faqs.answer: General: 21-30 years. OBC: 21-33 years (3 years relaxation). SC/ST: 21-35 years (5 years relaxation). PwBD: 21-40 years (10 years relaxation). Age is calculated as on the notification date. All relaxations are as per Government of India guidelines.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SBI PO and SBI Clerk?
faqs.answer: SBI PO is an officer-level job starting at ₹48-50K/month. SBI Clerk is a junior cadre role starting at ₹26-30K/month. PO has management duties and faster promotion to branch manager level. Clerk mostly handles customer transactions. PO exam is harder (descriptive paper + interview) than Clerk (objective only).

faqs.question: Can I clear SBI PO without coaching?
faqs.answer: Yes, many candidates clear SBI PO through self-study. Required: 6-8 months of consistent prep, good online mock tests (Oliveboard, Testbook, Adda247), standard books, and daily current affairs. The key is regular mock test practice — 50+ full-length mocks before the exam. Coaching helps with motivation and doubt-solving but isn't essential.

faqs.question: What is the age limit for SBI PO 2026?
faqs.answer: Age limit is 21-30 years as of April 1, 2026. Relaxations: OBC (+3 years, 21-33), SC/ST (+5 years, 21-35), PwD (+10-15 years), Ex-servicemen (+5 years). Maximum 4 attempts for General, 7 for OBC, unlimited for SC/ST within age limit.

syllabus.subject: Reasoning & Computer Aptitude
syllabus.topics: Seating arrangement (linear, circular, floor-based), puzzles (scheduling, box, comparison), syllogism, coding-decoding, blood relations, input-output machine, data sufficiency, computer fundamentals

syllabus.subject: Data Analysis & Interpretation
syllabus.topics: Bar graph, line graph, pie chart, tabular DI, caselet DI, missing DI, data sufficiency, probability, permutation & combination

syllabus.subject: English Language
syllabus.topics: Reading comprehension (2 passages), cloze test, error detection, sentence rearrangement, fill in blanks, column matching, vocabulary-based questions

syllabus.subject: General/Economy/Banking Awareness
syllabus.topics: Banking terms, RBI policies, budget highlights, financial institutions, current affairs (6 months), awards, summits, appointments, government schemes

study_plan.tasks: SBI PO is slightly harder than IBPS PO. Focus on advanced DI (caselet, missing). Banking awareness is crucial — read about RBI monetary policy, Basel norms, financial inclusion. English: 2 RC passages daily.
Complex puzzles — 5 sets daily (floor + scheduling combos). Descriptive English practice — SBI Mains has essay + letter writing. Start taking sectional tests. Current affairs compilation.
3 full mocks per week. Descriptive paper practice — write 1 essay + 1 letter daily. Group discussion topics preparation. Current affairs and banking awareness revision. Target: 100+ in Prelims.
